questions.question: Which HTML element is used to define the main navigation links on a page?
options: <div class="nav">
<navigation>
<nav>
<menu>

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of using semantic HTML elements like <article> and <section>?
options: They make pages load faster
They provide meaning to content for browsers and assistive technologies
They automatically add styling to the page
They are required for valid HTML5 documents

questions.question: Which attribute should always be included in the <html> tag for accessibility?
options: charset
dir
lang
role

questions.question: What does the CSS property 'box-sizing: border-box' do?
options: Adds a border around all elements
Makes padding and border included in the element's total width and height
Removes all box shadows from the element
Sets the element's display to block

questions.question: In CSS specificity, which selector has the HIGHEST priority?
options: Element selector (e.g., p)
Class selector (e.g., .intro)
ID selector (e.g., #header)
Universal selector (*)

questions.question: Why should you use relative units (rem, em, %) instead of fixed pixels in CSS?
options: Pixels are not supported by modern browsers
Relative units ensure layout elasticity and better responsiveness across devices
Relative units make the website load faster
Fixed pixels cause accessibility violations

questions.question: What is a CSS Custom Property (CSS variable) and how is it defined?
options: A JavaScript variable used in stylesheets, defined with 'let'
A special property defined with '--' prefix and accessed with 'var()'
A property defined in the HTML style attribute only
A built-in browser property that cannot be customised

questions.question: Which JavaScript method is the recommended way to handle user interactions instead of inline HTML event attributes?
options: document.write()
element.innerHTML
element.addEventListener()
window.alert()

questions.question: What is the DOM (Document Object Model)?
options: A CSS framework for styling documents
A programming interface that represents the page as a tree of objects that scripts can manipulate
A server-side database for storing web page data
A file format for storing HTML documents

questions.question: What does a 'pure function' in JavaScript mean?
options: A function that only uses ES6 syntax
A function that never returns a value
A function that returns consistent results for the same inputs and has no side effects
A function that modifies global variables

questions.question: Which HTML element is used to provide a caption for an image?
options: <caption>
<figcaption> inside a <figure>
<img-caption>
<label>

questions.question: What is the correct way to prevent 'div-soup' in HTML?
options: Use only <span> elements instead of <div>
Replace generic <div> elements with semantic elements like <header>, <main>, <aside>
Add ARIA roles to every <div> element
Limit the number of <div> elements to 10 per page

questions.question: What does the CSS 'cascade' refer to?
options: A waterfall animation effect
The algorithm that determines which styles apply when multiple rules target the same element
The order in which CSS files are downloaded
A method of nesting CSS selectors

questions.question: Which method is used to select an HTML element by its ID in JavaScript?
options: document.getElement(id)
document.querySelector(id)
document.getElementById(id)
document.findById(id)

questions.question: Why is input validation important in JavaScript?
options: It makes the code run faster
It prevents security vulnerabilities like XSS and ensures data integrity
It is required by the JavaScript specification
It only affects the visual appearance of forms
